solution: ```class Solution:
    def mergeAlternately(self, word1: str, word2: str) -> str:
        res = []
        i = 0
        while i < len(word1) or i < len(word2):
            if i < len(word1):
                res.append(word1[i])
            if i < len(word2):
                res.append(word2[i])
            i += 1
        return ''.join(res)
```
efficiency: HIGH
time complexity: O(n + m)
space complexity: O(n + m)
energy implications: LOW
explanation: Iterate once through both strings simultaneously, appending characters alternately. When one string ends, append the remainder of the other. Uses a list for efficient concatenation and a single pass.
